description_long: This is a rectangular, mid-rise apartment building with a flat, weathered concrete roof featuring a small rooftop structure and a vent. The exterior is clad in reddish-brown brickwork, with regularly spaced rectangular windows that have dark frames and some with visible bars. The front facade includes a recessed entryway sheltered by a concrete awning supported by columns, and the main entrance is accessible via a short staircase. The building has two main floors, with the ground floor featuring larger windows and a more prominent entry area, while the upper floor windows are smaller and more uniform. The side and rear facades are simpler, with fewer windows and minimal decorative elements, emphasizing functionality over ornamentation. The overall appearance suggests a utilitarian, early-to-mid 20th-century urban residential structure commonly found in city neighborhoods.
description: A rectangular, brick-clad apartment building with a flat, weathered roof, barred windows, and a recessed front entryway. The structure is utilitarian and urban, with two main floors and minimal decorative features.
description_view_0: This view shows the front and left side, highlighting the main entrance with a concrete awning, barred windows, and the flat, cracked roof.
description_view_1: The rear and left side are visible, showing fewer windows and the continuation of the brick facade, with the rooftop vent and structure more prominent.
description_view_2: The back and right side are shown, featuring a simple brick wall with a single arched doorway and a staircase, and minimal window openings.
description_view_3: This view displays the right side and front, emphasizing the side staircase, arched doorway, and the front entry area with its awning.
attribute_window_arrangement: Windows are symmetrically arranged in rows on each floor, with even spacing and alignment, and some windows feature bars. The front facade has more windows, while the sides and rear have fewer and are more sparsely distributed.
